fact: June 10 is exactly 161 days into the year, which means there are exactly 204 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, the sun rises super early around June 10 - sometimes before 5:30 AM in places like New York!
Fireflies start their crazy blinking light shows around this time in North America, turning backyards into natural dance parties!
June 10 falls smack in the middle of Gemini season, when people born under this zodiac sign are supposed to be extra chatty and curious.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, June 10 is deep in winter, so kangaroos grow their thickest, fluffiest fur coats right around now!
The sun sets after 8 PM in many northern cities on June 10, giving kids way more daylight hours to play outside after dinner!
Strawberry plants in North America are pumping out their juiciest, reddest berries right around this date - that's why June is called strawberry season!
June 10 is only 11 days away from the summer solstice, making it one of the longest days of the year in terms of sunlight!
In northern places like Alaska, the sun barely sets at all around June 10 - it just dips low and pops right back up like a yo-yo!
Mosquitoes are at their most annoying peak around this time in many places because the weather is finally warm and wet enough for them to party!
Ocean water temperatures in the Northern Hemisphere are just starting to warm up enough for comfortable swimming around June 10!
In the Southern Hemisphere, June 10 brings some of the coldest morning frosts of the year, turning spider webs into sparkly ice sculptures!
